description_long: This corded phone features a robust, curved main body with a glossy dark green finish and metallic reddish-brown accents on the base and cradle. The handset is suspended on a cradle at the front, connected to the main unit by a coiled cord, which is clearly visible from the side and rear views. The base is wide and stable, supporting the upright, slightly forward-leaning body. The front of the device displays a circular rotary dial with numbered finger holes, surrounded by a metallic ring. The phone's design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century public telephones, with a utilitarian yet stylish appearance. The materials suggest a combination of metal and plastic, with a sturdy, hard surface and a slight sheen.
description: A vintage-style corded phone with a curved, glossy green body, metallic reddish-brown base, and a rotary dial. The handset rests on a cradle and is attached by a coiled cord.
description_view_0: Front view shows the curved body, handset on cradle, and the coiled cord extending from the side.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the curvature of the body and the coiled cord connecting the handset to the base.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the rotary dial, the full circular base, and the attachment point of the coiled cord.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the handset profile and the phone's overall curvature.
